verdict.answer_first: B states the answer immediately: "A database index is a separate data structure...to speed up lookups"; A starts similarly after a heading but is slightly less direct.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives a stronger practical rule: "start with no indexes beyond your keys...then index deliberately"; B is clear but more general.
verdict.fabrication: A has an unsupported precise claim: "~20 comparisons and 1,000,000"; B has no comparable inflated figure.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it includes concrete signals and traps: "Run EXPLAIN...If you see a sequential/full table scan...that's your signal" plus function/composite/wildcard caveats.
